entity.summary: New Zealand centralized cryptocurrency exchange that suffered a major January 2019 hack and entered liquidation in May 2019 after it could not resume normal operations.
entity.notes: Death date follows the appointment of liquidators on 2019-05-14. The hack was the key preceding incident; insolvency/liquidation is used as the terminal reason because liquidation formally ended exchange operations.

events.title: Cryptopia launched
events.description: Cryptopia operated as a New Zealand-based cryptocurrency exchange founded in 2014.
events.notes: Year-level launch date based on public background references.

events.title: Major exchange hack
events.description: Cryptopia suffered a major theft in January 2019, with later reporting describing a large loss of exchange-held crypto assets.
events.notes: Preceding incident that pushed the exchange toward liquidation.

events.title: Liquidators appointed
events.description: Grant Thornton New Zealand was appointed liquidator of Cryptopia Limited after the hacked exchange could not resume normal business operations.
events.notes: Used as death_date because liquidation formally ended the exchange as an operating platform.

evidence.title: Cryptopia company history reference
evidence.notes: Used only for basic launch-year and entity-background cross-checking.

evidence.title: Cryptopia Exchange Goes Offline After Hack
evidence.notes: Used for the January 2019 hack and offline status.

evidence.title: Cryptopia exchange hack estimate and impact reference
evidence.notes: Used as a secondary source for hack impact and asset-loss context.

evidence.title: Cryptopia Limited in liquidation
evidence.notes: Primary liquidator notice for the 2019-05-14 liquidation event.

evidence.title: Cryptopia liquidation overview
evidence.notes: Liquidation portal and ongoing official reference for account-holder process.
